sentence_reviews.text: Formal agent verification checks plans, policies, tool sequences, or generated programs against state, temporal, safety, and authorization specifications.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Formal ML survey supports formal specifications and verification; AIMA supports planning/state/action formalisms; structured-output survey supports constrained/typed generation.
sentence_reviews.notes: Defensible synthesis across sources; authorization/tool-sequence specifics are not directly established by one source.

sentence_reviews.text: It adds a machine-checkable control layer between an agent’s proposal and an external side effect.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Formal methods provide machine-checkable specification satisfaction; structured outputs can constrain generated actions before execution.
sentence_reviews.notes: Plausible synthesis for agent safety, not directly verified.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Formal methods survey covers temporal/safety specifications; AIMA covers planning formalisms.
sentence_reviews.notes: Supported as a standard formal-planning verification example.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Formal methods support proof obligations; structured output generation supports constrained generated artifacts, but proof-carrying code generation is not directly sourced.
sentence_reviews.notes: Plausible but needs a proof-carrying-code or verified synthesis source.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Formal specifications can express state and safety properties; permission-state authorization is not directly covered by the packet source.

sentence_reviews.text: Open-world observations and underspecified environments make complete verification difficult, so runtime monitoring remains necessary.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Formal ML survey discusses specification/model limitations and scalability; NIST supports ongoing monitoring and risk management.
sentence_reviews.notes: Well supported as synthesis; runtime monitoring necessity is governance reasoning.

sentence_reviews.text: Formal verification of agents & plans measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Formal methods, specifications & proof obligations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Formal verification depends on formal specifications and proof obligations.

sentence_reviews.text: Formal verification of agents & plans checks candidate behavior or artifacts against evidence or constraints from PDDL, STRIPS & hierarchical task-network planning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA covers STRIPS/PDDL-style planning and hierarchical planning formalisms for action/state plans.

sentence_reviews.text: Formal verification of agents & plans checks candidate behavior or artifacts against evidence or constraints from Structured outputs & typed action generation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Structured-output generation survey supports constrained/typed generation from LLMs, relevant to checkable action artifacts.
sentence_reviews.notes: Common dependency is plausible, not necessarily core.

sentence_reviews.text: Formal verification of agents & plans checks candidate behavior or artifacts against evidence or constraints from Code execution, interpreters & sandboxes.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Formal methods can verify generated programs/artifacts; sandboxed execution is not directly established by cited sources.
sentence_reviews.notes: Relationship is plausible for agent tool/code workflows but undersourced.

edge_reviews.notes: Specifications and proof obligations are core to formal verification.
Planning formalisms are core substrates for plan verification.
Typed/constrained outputs can support checkable agent artifacts; common strength is appropriate.
Generated programs can be verification targets, but code execution/sandbox dependence is not directly established.
